content.problemStatement: You are given an integer sequence AAA with length NNN. In one move, you are allowed to choose one element of this sequence and change it to an arbitrary integer. Compute the minimum number of moves needed to make the sequence a permutation of the integers 111 through NNN.
constraints: the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum required number of moves.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One possible way is to change the last element to 444. This creates the sequence 1,2,3,41, 2, 3, 41,2,3,4, which is a permutation.
Example case 2: We can change the first element to 111 and the second element to 333, creating the permutation 1,3,21, 3, 21,3,2.
